hero.badge: Danang, Hoi An & Ba Na Hills — 4 Days
hero.title: 4 Days in Central Vietnam.
hero.titleAccent: Golden Bridge, Ancient Town & Dragon Fire.
hero.intro: Three of Central Vietnam's most distinct experiences in four days. Ba Na Hills' Golden Bridge — two giant stone hands lifting a gold pedestrian walkway through mountain cloud at 1,400 metres. UNESCO Hoi An, the best-preserved ancient trading port in Southeast Asia. And Da Nang's Dragon Bridge, breathing fire and water on weekend nights. Central Vietnam's greatest hits, arranged properly.

overview.heading: 4 Days Central Vietnam: Danang, Ba Na Hills & Hoi An
body: This four-day package covers the three sites that define Central Vietnam for most visitors from Malaysia: the extraordinary mountain resort of Ba Na Hills, the UNESCO-listed Ancient Town of Hoi An, and Da Nang's coastline and city — all from a single hotel base in Da Nang.

Ba Na Hills occupies a 1,487-metre ridge 25 kilometres west of Da Nang. The French built it as a hill station in the 1920s; Sun World rebuilt it in 2009 as Vietnam's most ambitious theme resort. The Golden Bridge — opened in 2018, designed with two enormous stone hands lifting a pedestrian walkway through the clouds — has become the most photographed image in Vietnam and one of the most shared in the world. The all-inclusive Sun World combo covers the cable car, the French village, all attractions, and the buffet lunch.

Hoi An, 30 kilometres south of Da Nang, is the most intact historic trading port in Southeast Asia. Its Old Town — lantern-lit, traffic-free, and remarkably preserved — was a stopping point for Chinese, Japanese, Dutch, and Portuguese merchants from the 15th to 17th centuries. The architecture reflects all of them. The bamboo basket boat ride at Cam Thanh Village, a wetland ecosystem just outside the town, provides a different angle on the local fishing culture that has sustained the community for centuries.

highlights.heading: Why This 4-Day Central Vietnam Package Stands Apart

items.title: Golden Bridge — Ba Na Hills' Global Icon
items.body: Opened in 2018, the Golden Bridge at Ba Na Hills has been shared more than almost any image from Southeast Asia — two enormous weathered stone hands lifting a gilded pedestrian walkway 1,400 metres above sea level, through mountain cloud that drifts across the path. The effect is theatrical and genuinely startling in person. The Sun World resort around it includes a French village, a fantasy garden, cable car with panoramic views across the Da Nang coastline, and a 12th-century château recreation that wouldn't look out of place in Burgundy.
items.imageAlt: Golden Bridge Ba Na Hills stone hands pedestrian walkway Vietnam

items.title: Hoi An Ancient Town — UNESCO Living Heritage
items.body: Hoi An was the most important trading port in Southeast Asia from the 15th to 18th centuries — Chinese, Japanese, Dutch, and Portuguese merchants built their own quarters in the town and left their architectural fingerprints. The Japanese Covered Bridge, built in 1593, links the Japanese and Chinese quarters over a small canal. Tan Ky Old House (200 years old) has Japanese balcony carvings on the ground floor and Chinese plasterwork on the upper. The Fujian Assembly Hall was built by the Fujian Chinese community in 1757. The guided walking tour covers all of this with context that makes the layers of history legible.
items.imageAlt: Hoi An Ancient Town lanterns Japanese Bridge UNESCO Vietnam

items.title: Basket Boat Ride — Cam Thanh Village
items.body: The traditional coracle — a round woven bamboo boat sealed with resin — is one of the most distinctive craft of the Hoi An fishing community, originally developed to evade French colonial fishing taxes (round boats had no clear bow or stern and therefore couldn't be classified as taxable fishing vessels). The Cam Thanh coconut palm wetland, 3 kilometres from Hoi An, is the place to experience it: local fishermen navigate the narrow water channels through nipa palm forest, then spin the boat in place as a demonstration of the technique that takes years to master.
items.imageAlt: Cam Thanh Village basket boat coracle ride Hoi An coconut palm

items.title: Dragon Bridge — Weekend Fire & Water Show
items.body: Da Nang's Dragon Bridge is a 666-metre pedestrian and vehicle crossing over the Han River, shaped as a yellow dragon with a head and tail on opposite banks. Every Friday, Saturday, and Sunday at 9pm, the dragon breathes fire and sprays water from its mouth — a spectacle visible from the riverside promenade and surrounding restaurants. It is both a genuine piece of infrastructure (cars drive over it daily) and Da Nang's most crowd-pleasing evening ritual.
items.imageAlt: Dragon Bridge Danang fire water show weekend Vietnam

itinerary.heading: Your 4-Day Danang + Hoi An + Ba Na Hills Itinerary

days.title: Arrive Danang — City Tour & Dragon Bridge
days.description: Arrive at Da Nang International Airport, welcomed by your guide and transferred to your hotel. Lunch at Bep Cuon Restaurant (My Quang noodle, a Central Vietnamese specialty not widely available outside the region). Afternoon city tour: Marble Mountains (five marble hills containing Buddhist shrines, caves, and mountain viewpoints — each named for a natural element), Monkey Mountain at Son Tra Peninsula and Linh Ung Bai But Pagoda (a 67-metre white Buddha overlooking the sea — the largest Lady Buddha statue in Vietnam), and the Pink Church (Da Nang Cathedral). Evening: Love Bridge over the Han River and Dragon Bridge photo stop. If your arrival day is Friday, Saturday, or Sunday, the Dragon Bridge fire and water show begins at 9pm.
suggestedActivities: My Quang lunch at Bep Cuon Restaurant
Marble Mountains (marble-and-limestone hills with caves and shrines)
Son Tra Peninsula — Monkey Mountain viewpoint
Linh Ung Bai But Pagoda (67m Lady Buddha)
Love Bridge and Dragon Bridge evening walk

days.title: Sun World Ba Na Hills — Golden Bridge & French Village
days.description: Early morning departure for Ba Na Hills Mountain Resort (45 km, approximately 1 hour). The Sun World combo package includes all cable car rides (the highest and longest gondola system in Vietnam), all attraction entries, the funicular railway up to the peak, and buffet lunch at the resort's restaurant. Visit the Golden Bridge — the two-hands walkway above the clouds — the French Village with its 1920s hill-station architecture and château tower, and the Fantasy Park indoor amusement area. Pickup at 4pm; return to Da Nang. Evening stop at Han Market or Vincom Shopping Mall. BBQ hotpot dinner. Optional: Dragon Bridge spitfire show (Fri/Sat/Sun at 9pm).
suggestedActivities: Ba Na Hills cable car (panoramic Da Nang coast views)
Golden Bridge — two stone hands walkway

days.title: Hoi An Ancient Town — Basket Boat & UNESCO Walking Tour
days.description: Morning transfer to Cam Thanh Village (coconut palm wetland on the Thu Bon River) for the traditional bamboo basket boat (coracle) experience — local fishermen navigate water channels through nipa palm forest and demonstrate the spinning technique unique to Hoi An's fishing culture. Transfer to UNESCO World Heritage-listed Hoi An Ancient Town (30 km from Da Nang). Guided walking tour with entry fees covering: Japanese Covered Bridge (1593), Tan Ky Old House (200 years of Chinese-Japanese architectural layering), Fujian Assembly Hall (1757, built by the Fujian Chinese community), and the Museum of Trade Ceramics. Free time for tailoring, shopping, and coffee in the ancient town. Return to Da Nang by 4pm.
suggestedActivities: Cam Thanh Village basket boat (coracle) ride
Hoi An Ancient Town walking tour
Free time: tailors, lantern shopping, cafés

days.title: Danang Free Morning — Depart
days.description: After breakfast, free time for self-arranged activities: My Khe Beach (one of Asia's most-cited beaches — 30 km of white sand, gentle waves, and clear water just 5 minutes from the city centre), final souvenir shopping, or Han Market. Transfer to Da Nang International Airport for your departure.

whyBook.heading: Central Vietnam — Arranged by Malaysia's Trusted Agency Since 1988
whyBook.body: Central Vietnam is one of Dhesu's most popular destinations for Malaysian travellers. Our guides know the Ba Na Hills timings (the Golden Bridge is clearest early morning before the cloud builds), which areas of Hoi An to visit first, and how to navigate the basket boat operators who give the most authentic experience. Every detail is arranged so your four days are completely smooth.

points.title: MATTA & IATA Registered
points.body: Fully licensed Malaysian travel agency. Central Vietnam is one of our most booked circuits — we understand every logistical detail from Ba Na Hills to Hoi An.

points.title: 35+ Years of Vietnam Experience
points.body: Since 1988, we have been arranging Vietnam journeys for Malaysians. Our Da Nang guides are trusted long-term partners, not rotating contractors.

points.title: Ba Na Hills Sun World Included
points.body: The full Sun World combo (cable car, all attractions, funicular, buffet lunch) is included in the package price. No queuing separately for each ticket.

points.title: Transparent Pricing
points.body: The Buy 3 Free 1 promotion applies — groups of 4 pay for 3. All entry fees, cable car charges, and basket boat rides are included where specified.

sections.heading: 4 Day Danang Hoi An Ba Na Hills Tour from Malaysia
sections.body:
A 4-day Da Nang, Hoi An, and Ba Na Hills tour from Malaysia is the most efficient way to experience Central Vietnam's three signature attractions. Da Nang is the gateway — a coastal city with excellent flight connections from Kuala Lumpur, a good hotel infrastructure, and enough of its own attractions (Marble Mountains, Dragon Bridge, My Khe Beach) to warrant a day of its own. Ba Na Hills, 45 kilometres inland, has become Vietnam's most photographed destination since the Golden Bridge opened in 2018. Hoi An, 30 kilometres south, is the destination that makes Central Vietnam a complete experience.

The four-day format works because all three destinations are within 45 kilometres of Da Nang — there is no long inter-city transit. You stay in one hotel throughout and day-trip to Ba Na Hills and Hoi An on separate days. This is the most popular Central Vietnam itinerary for Malaysian visitors.

sections.heading: Is the Ba Na Hills Sun World Cable Car Worth It?
sections.body: The Ba Na Hills cable car is included in the Sun World combo and is part of what makes the resort remarkable: the gondola system holds multiple world records (including steepest gradient and longest single-rope section) and the views across the Da Nang coastline, visible through the cloud on the ascent, are outstanding. The Golden Bridge at the top — the most-shared travel image from Vietnam in 2018 and 2019 — is genuinely impressive in person. The French Village and château reconstruction that surround it are theatrical in a way that either delights or amuses, depending on your tolerance for theme resort aesthetics. The buffet lunch is hearty. Most visitors spend 5–7 hours on the mountain.

items.question: Is the Golden Bridge at Ba Na Hills worth visiting?
items.answer: Yes — the Golden Bridge is genuinely impressive in person and very different from the photographs. The scale of the stone hands, the cloud that drifts across the walkway at 1,400 metres, and the mountain views below it combine to create a setting unlike anything else in Southeast Asia. It is crowded at peak times (Vietnamese public holidays, weekends from 10am–2pm), but the resort's size means the crowds are absorbed. The best time to visit is early morning (before 9am) when the cloud cover is at its most atmospheric and the visitor numbers are lower.

items.question: How far is Hoi An from Da Nang?
items.answer: Approximately 30 kilometres by road, taking around 45 minutes by private vehicle. The drive south follows the coast past My Khe Beach and the Marble Mountains. Your guide and vehicle are included for the Hoi An day trip — no separate transport arrangement is needed.

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for Vietnam?
items.answer: No. Malaysian passport holders can enter Vietnam visa-free for up to 30 days. You arrive at Da Nang International Airport and pass through immigration with your Malaysian passport — no advance visa application required.

items.question: Can I get clothes tailor-made in Hoi An?
items.answer: Yes. Hoi An is one of the best tailoring destinations in the world — dozens of tailor shops can replicate any garment from a photograph or existing piece in 24–48 hours, at prices far below comparable quality anywhere else. For a 4-day package, you would need to visit a tailor on Day 3 and arrange same-day or next-morning collection. Your guide can recommend reliable tailors who deliver on time.

items.question: What is the Dragon Bridge fire show schedule?
items.answer: The Dragon Bridge fire and water show takes place every Friday, Saturday, and Sunday at 9pm. The bridge breathes fire from its mouth and sprays water, with the spectacle visible from the Han River promenade and adjacent restaurants. If your Da Nang arrival or a free evening falls on these days, the show is worth watching — it takes approximately 15 minutes and draws a significant crowd.
